description: Occupy Wall Street began September 17, 2011 in Zuccotti Park, NYC. Support rose from ~40% to peak ~50% in October as 'We are the 99%' resonated, then faded to ~30% by December as camps were cleared and lack of concrete demands drew criticism. Tests protest movement lifecycle and media fatigue.

key_events.description: Occupy Wall Street begins Sept 17 in Zuccotti Park. 'We are the 99%' slogan goes viral. Initial media coverage treats as curiosity.
NYPD mass arrest on Brooklyn Bridge (700 arrested). Police overreaction generates sympathy and massive media coverage. Support surges.
Occupy spreads to 600+ communities worldwide. Unions endorse movement. Peak support ~50% in Quinnipiac/CBS polls.
Zuccotti Park evicted by NYPD (Nov 15). Other camps cleared nationwide. Media narrative shifts to disorder, hygiene, lack of demands.
Movement largely dissipated by year end. Criticism of leaderless structure and lack of policy demands dominates coverage. Support drops to ~30%.

calibration_notes: Tests protest movement rise-and-fall lifecycle. Key patterns: classic bell curve trajectory (unique among calibration scenarios), very high initial undecided (55%) indicating low awareness, police overreaction as catalyst for sympathy, peak reached in ~6 weeks, decline driven by media fatigue + negative framing (disorder, no demands), undecideds converting to opposition as movement faded, lasting legacy on income inequality discourse despite apparent failure. PPP, Quinnipiac, CBS/NYT polling data used.
